methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $95M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $1.0B - $2.5B.

methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 45.3x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Fortastra, a Series C space and aerospace company founded by Mike Smayda, was valued using two applicable methodologies against a comparable universe of five private peers (Axiom Space, Impulse Space, Varda Space Industries, Skyryse, Isar Aerospace) at the same stage, plus five public sector benchmarks (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL).

Stage-progression analysis applied a 1.7x step-up multiplier to an $95M total-raised proxy, yielding $162M. Peer-median valuation across the five same-stage, same-sector comparables produced $1.5B, with a range from $1.0B (Isar Aerospace) to $2.5B (Axiom Space). Public-comp anchoring was not applicable due to absence of target revenue data, though the sector median P/S multiple stands at 45.3x across the six public tickers surveyed.

The triangulated valuation range is $137M (low) to $1.7B (high), with a median of $831M. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty around Fortastra's relative market position within the Space & Aerospace frontier sector. Stage-progression generates a more conservative estimate, while peer-median pricing incorporates the elevated valuations observed among later-stage private entrants in this domain. Revenue disclosure from Fortastra would enable public-comp validation and materially narrow this range.
